name: hyperfine (CLI Benchmarking Tool)

performance.notes: Benchmarks other commands; overhead is minimal

agent_readiness.notes: Statistical CLI benchmarking tool. `hyperfine 'cmd1' 'cmd2'` compares commands. Warmup runs (--warmup). Min/max runs (--min-runs). Shell: --shell=none for overhead removal. --prepare for setup commands. JSON/Markdown/CSV export (--export-json). Relative speedup comparison. Progress bar. Statistical analysis: mean, stddev, min, max. Outlier detection. Rust-based from sharkdp. Used for comparing compiler flags, tool versions, algorithm implementations.

gotchas: Results affected by system load during benchmarks
Warmup runs required for accurate filesystem/cache benchmarks
best_when: Comparing performance of two commands or tool versions
Benchmarking shell scripts or CLI tools
avoid_when: Microbenchmarks in code (use language-native tools: pytest-benchmark, criterion)
summary: hyperfine is a statistical CLI benchmarking tool. `hyperfine 'cmd1' 'cmd2'` runs multiple iterations and reports mean, stddev, relative speedup. Warmup runs, JSON/Markdown export. Rust-based from the bat/fd author. Best for comparing two command implementations or measuring CLI performance impact. Used to benchmark compiler flags, tool versions, and shell script optimizations.
